---
title: "RR Strategy Research"
subtitle: "A systematic framework for multi-asset investing and risk premia harvesting"
author: "Juan Antonio Roger"
date: today
---

## About this document

This document builds a rigorous foundation for the kind of investment process that takes markets seriously — one that respects the difficulty of outperformance, understands where returns actually come from, and designs portfolios that can survive the conditions they will realistically encounter.

It draws on work from Fama, Cochrane, Campbell and Shiller, Ilmanen, Asness and colleagues at AQR, and Research Affiliates, alongside practical experience of risk management and portfolio oversight in live markets. Theory without implementation tends toward abstraction. Implementation without theory tends toward overconfidence in backtests. The framework attempts to hold both together.

## Structure

The framework begins with prices — what they are, why they embed expectations rather than reflect value directly, and how crowd dynamics can drive them far from any fundamental anchor. From there it asks whether that complexity can be turned into a consistent edge, and concludes that it rarely can at the aggregate level, though analytical work still earns its place in specific and identifiable ways.

The middle sections build the constructive case: where returns actually come from, how to combine them into a portfolio that survives the conditions it will encounter, and how to estimate what that portfolio should earn from its current starting point rather than from a historical average. The final section before the strategy addresses the organizational hierarchy — core allocation comes first, active additions require explicit justification, and conviction under adversity is as important as the analytical work that preceded the allocation.

The seventh section applies all of this to a specific strategy. It documents what the strategy does, what the backtest evidence shows across different parameter configurations and macro regimes, and what the early live track record on Darwinex Zero can and cannot yet tell us. The standards against which the strategy is evaluated are the ones the preceding sections established — not retrospectively, but by design.

## The connected strategy

The ES Core Relative Allocation maintains U.S. large-cap equity futures as a permanent core and dynamically allocates up to 50 percent of the portfolio to alternative futures instruments based on their risk-adjusted performance relative to that core. It trades six USD-denominated futures contracts — ES, NQ, RTY, GC, SI, and ZN — and is deployed live on Darwinex Zero under the ticker UJNU.

A strategy without a framework is a backtest with no explanation of why it should continue to work. A framework without a strategy is a literature review with no practical consequence. Together they form a documented investment process that can be evaluated and improved on the basis of evidence.

## References and charts

Citations refer to publicly available academic papers, working papers, and practitioner research. Charts are drawn from the Ken French Data Library, Robert Shiller's data series, FRED, Yahoo Finance, and S&P Dow Jones Indices publications. Several chart propositions remain as marked placeholders where the required data is not yet available and will be populated as the strategy analysis is completed.
